transcript: Carla is happier that I'm leaving. She cries very little, but she'll miss me. You know who will be happier? [Laughs] New things in a suitcase to wash, she already... I haven't even left yet and she's already started packing my things for Marcela when the farewell comes. [Laughs] You'll be there for five months, be very happy, your stay there will be fruitful. My... a little bit of the flesh... Look at you, who are 24 years old. Who said I'm not 24? It 's Maria Clara trying to make me look older. For someone who is 24, five months is a lot, and for someone who is... [Music] it can be an eternity. I would like to remind you that life cries out for help, even people, animals too, because faith is lacking. I hope you take a little bit of faith from all of us here because you may need it. There's a lack of goodwill, a lack of care, a lack of the arrival of a very important person, a lack of... You can't make a mistake, the most important person at this moment to accompany your little sister, huh? So God is missing, but the affection of each of us in the happiest moments is not missing. Remember us, we will be involved and...  Much love, and may God also be taking care of you. He is preparing your path for sure. Don't let anyone tell you that there are thorns, because there are still many who seek them. You know, every time we seek thorns, every time we seek spirits, God seeks them when we don't have negative thoughts. Today, what is studied in academia is energy, and energy is action, and action moves. So we have two possibilities, and you will have two: you can choose negative thoughts or positive thoughts. Whoever believes in God, whoever has positive thoughts is getting closer to God. Because we usually say that people who believe in God are... Gabi, love, love. Every time we feel that love is lacking in our lives, we have to connect with the only certainty we have here. We said that affection is lacking, that harmony is lacking, that sensitivity from people is lacking. And then we have to think, why is that lacking? Does it depend on others? My joy says no, because when God created us, He created us so that we would be happy regardless of other people. Imagine if it depended on a son, a daughter, a friend for us to be happy. When my son, when my friend, when other people are happy, I will be... Happy? God gave us the capacity to experience peace and harmony, regardless of the people around us. We talk about thorns because some still exist. But one thing you can't lose, and I think you'll never lose, is faith. Is it faith in your father, in your mother, my friends? Do you know the answer? [Music] Worried, you'll never be crying, and I would like you to take Psalm 23 with you, take it with you as a piece of each of us. Psalm 23 says: " The Lord is my shepherd ; the Lord is my God." This means relationship. This means harmony; nothing shall be lacking, nothing is provision. Walking makes me walk through green pastures. Walking through open spaces is rest. If we want your life, all of us, and rest in still waters, rest in still waters. It's care, care that your mother, that I dedicated some moments to, and your mother always, and your friends too sometimes, but this care will be forever because when God cares, He refreshes my soul. Do you know what it is to refresh the soul? It's healing. He guides me in the paths of righteousness.  When he's walking in righteousness, that's direction, that's the best path, for the love of His name, that's purpose, and God's purpose is the best purpose, even though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death. You know what it's like to walk through difficult moments in our lives, that's a trial. And the trial will show our strength, more than our strength, the approval, the difficulties, the pain, the despair, the heartbreak, you know what that is? It's a trial. I will fear no evil, you know what it means to ask ourselves, to have faith. And there's a saying that goes, you show your faith, you sound like a pastor, because, buddy, I do n't know why, I show my works, these things are so distant, right? But that's life, that's peace that we don't find anywhere else. Your rod and your staff comfort me. When you're comforting someone, what are you bringing? Marcelo, hope is more than love, because when you have hope, you don't lose, because love is love, it's forever. [Music] Things are momentary, they pass, love remains, because when we love, we don't expect any answer, because love completes itself, and when  Love is complete in our lives, life will be peaceful. Today my head is anointed with oil, that is consecration, my cup overflows, which is something that overflows in our lives. What is that? Surely goodness and mercy will follow me all the days of my life. Oh, if mercy, if blessing, participates in your life and will always participate, do you know what that is? It is the blessing of God, and the blessing of God is that we are all now with our hearts, embracing your heart so that you, beyond the distance, have the presence of God. I will dwell securely in the house of the Lord, and if the promise is for long days, that is, eternity, the blessings of God are infinite in our lives. There are two words after that: believe and trust. I'm finishing, I'm going to tell you what it is for you, right? This capacity, not to tell you about the religion you should follow. I'm 24 years old, I'm approaching 80, and one day this happens, I reach 80 and you reach 25, close, right? And to talk about the best religion in the world, I brought Mother Teresa of Calcutta and Mother  Mother Teresa, regardless of religion, spoke like this: one day she received a man who was dying in a garbage can. She did this often, and he hadn't bathed in 30 years, which isn't your case. Bathing in the middle of the street, he had a moment of lucidity. This man was dying and asked the beloved lady, "Who is your God?" Look at the question: "Who is your God? Tell me who your God is so that I may love you, ma'am. But I am a man, I am a sinner." There's one more thing before I ask this, ma'am: "Who is your God? Tell me so that I may follow your God." And do you know what the beloved answered? She had been there for 30 years and said, " My God is you, my God is you." But ma'am, I am a sinful man. So tell me, what is your religion?" She was showing him that he wanted to know something, but what is your religion? Do you know what she answered? If her God was him, what was her religion? He asked her, and she answered, " My religion is you." But ma'am, I want to follow your religion, follow your conscience. My religion is you, but...  "Madam, what should I do to gain Nirvana? What should I do to gain heaven, to gain a better life on the other side? If that's what we're looking for, a safer destiny, a destiny of peace, I wanted to have that." I was talking to Mari, and she said, "Madam, what would I like to do to gain all this? There's only one thing, and that thing, Gabi, answers: what should I do to gain this heaven, to gain this hope on the other side? Regardless of religion, what should I do? You have to know how to follow the commandments." [Music] Whoever has a Bible, I don't know, whoever goes to church knows when it talks about love, because once they asked Jesus, "He doesn't even know. I don't know if young people believe in Jesus, I don't know how this relationship is so difficult to believe in, a person who was born more than two thousand years ago, when he does n't believe in the person who is by our side, right?" It's very difficult. And they asked Jesus what it was, look, Doctor of the Law, his Doctor, that person I knew from that time, asked Jesus, "What was the greatest commandment of the law that existed until then?" And Jesus answered, " The greatest commandment of all time is to love."  God is above all things, but he spoke a fundamental word according to his understanding. Because we cannot love God, but rather, you have to love God with all understanding of everything that we find most important here, but according to your understanding, and that's how it happens. And he says a second commandment, which is equal to this one, so it's also considered the first: love your neighbor, as [Music] itself, and that's it. That's what we need, to love our neighbor. And where is our neighbor? Ask yourself that. The neighbor begins, the neighbor begins here inside us, because we don't love ourselves, we don't like ourselves, we don't have the emotional capacity and balance to like the other. It's natural, it 's a whirlwind of, I don't know, some deficiencies. How am I going to have that love to love, to understand my neighbor? Because this love taught by Jesus, he's not asking for reciprocation. Brother, only you love, you did your part. That reciprocation is already a demand, and a demand is not love, and by any other name, but a demand is not love. That's it. But ma'am, I'm dying! Then he answers, oh, love while you die, to continue loving afterward. You don't love while you're dying, you'll like later, how will you accept later?  So, I 'm finished, so we're all here enveloping you with our best. This is a baggage you'll carry, you'll carry it in your noblest organ, which is the heart. The youngest speaks softly because the heart is the noblest because it is feeling, and feeling... Is there anything better than love? It's feeling, because feeling leads us to love, leads us to understanding, and that's what we're enveloping you with. You'll go with the immense baggage of our best. Look here, this is our family, my family, by blood, not even the one we respect. Look into our eyes, accept it as it is, I don't want to change it. Just one more thing, I'll talk about Jesus. We like to change people. The boyfriend has to do exactly what we want, the girlfriend has to do it the way we want. When Jesus found a person who was being stoned, I had done something that at the time was a horrible crime. She was being stoned, and we all know a story. Jesus says, "Take away the first stone, for he is without sin," and people left first. If it were today, young people would leave first because young people today have much more knowledge, much more experience, and I mean much more love. Then the elders were all there, and Jesus looked at her and said, "Has anyone condemned you?" She replied, "No, neither do I condemn you." Look, Jesus, who had all knowledge, didn't condemn anyone every day. And when we condemn, we create turmoil within our lives, our relationships, our intimate lives, and that brings unrest. But we are now enveloping ourselves in peace. We are now visualizing the best flower we can imagine, each with varied colors, bringing them all here before you. We are here for 30, 40, but there are always... Because some visualized, I think one of them, a better memory, and bringing it to you, you embrace it. [Applause]

quem aí também ama comida chinesa assim como eu o Marcelo até gosta de hoje no Restaurante Taiwan que é referência na culinária chinesa Tradicional em Londrina e servem as gerações há mais de 50 anos no cardápio são mais de 70 opções de pratos e eles contam também com os executivos que são servidos de segunda a sexta assim que você senta eles trazem a saladinha de repolho chinesa que é muito gostosa e nós pedimos várias vezes de entrada Nós escolhemos o guioza Frito que é delicioso ele recheado com carne de porco temperado com gengibre Cebolinha e também acompanha um olhinho e pra quem gosta de uma Pimentinha eles fazem na casa um molho de pimenta que é sensacional e eu como com todos os pratos de prato principal nós pedimos yakisoba Imperial que é feito com massa caseira carne de boi e legumes costela de boi Angus frita ou alho e óleo com Cebolinha e shoyu que é o meu prato favorito não pode faltar a roxa eu suei que é o yakimeshi que é um arroz temperado com presunto ovo cenoura e a pescada alho e óleo que a gente adora e ela vem inteira frita nós fomos em família é um ambiente Super agradável e é um lugar que meus pais me levam desde pequena de sobremesa nós pedimos Harumaki que é o rolinho primavera doce de banana com canela que vem uma banana inteira dentro e o de goiabada com queijo e cobertura de chocolate que na minha opinião é o melhor de todos já manda esse vídeo para um amigo que precisa conhecer o restaurante taiwa com você depois me contem e Me sigam para mais experiências como essa

hambúrguer com catupiry empanado batata com infusão de queijo hambúrguer com piscininha de cheddar Marcela degusta de hoje é no Vila melty Burgers and dreams e o lema da casa é uma infusão de sabores a cada mordida a Hamburgueria conta com o ambiente interno e externo que foi onde Nós escolhemos ficar e uma das novidades é que agora os pedidos são feitos através do tablet e além de ser super divertido e prático você consegue ver foto de todas as opções do cardápio começamos com a batata frita Queen com coberta com infusão cremosa de queijo cheddar e pepperoni que estava simplesmente deliciosa e a nossa segunda escolha de entrada foi o bolinho sem massa recheado com costela desfiada e catupiry com toda a certeza virou um dos meus queridinhos eles possuem mais de 10 opções de drinks e nós experimentamos o vergonha na cara um novo feito com Jack Daniel's e a kype Corona todos são muito gostosos mas o vergonha na cara ganhou o nosso coração inclusive eles fazem até uma brincadeira quando chega na mesa o hambúrguer mais pedido é o mel de Catupiry leva um disco de Catupiry empanado a carne é Angus esse hambúrguer é sensacional nós pedimos também o hambúrguer do mês que vem uma piscininha de cheddar com farofa de bacon limitada muito gostoso e todos os meses tem um diferente finalizar OS hambúrgueres ou infusão de gorgonzola que o toque especial dele a pimenta jalapeño e sobremesa nós pedimos o cheesecake de frutas vermelhas que é imperdível e a famosa torta de manteiga escocesa do Picapau e eu sempre quis provar e adorei e manda para um amigo que precisa conhecer o Villa Melody com você e depois me conta
